title: src/core/event.rs: nested map decoders allow duplicate keys; spec 3.1 requires rejecting duplicates

description:
**Problem**
Plan requires fuzz testing for decode paths to find edge cases.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §18 (fuzzing + crash safety)

**Design**
- cargo-fuzz targets:
  - WAL frame decode (length/crc/tail truncation)
  - CBOR EventBody decode with bounds (depth, map entries, array entries)
  - Replication message decode (HELLO/WELCOME/EVENTS)
- Use libfuzzer or afl via cargo-fuzz
- CI runs fuzz for bounded time on each PR (optional)

**Acceptance**
- [ ] fuzz/fuzz_targets/wal_decode.rs exists
- [ ] fuzz/fuzz_targets/event_body_decode.rs exists
- [ ] fuzz/fuzz_targets/repl_message_decode.rs exists
- [ ] cargo fuzz run <target> works

**Files:** fuzz/fuzz_targets/wal_decode.rs (new), fuzz/fuzz_targets/event_body_decode.rs (new), fuzz/fuzz_targets/repl_message_decode.rs (new), Cargo.toml
title: Cross-phase: Fuzz targets for WAL/CBOR/replication decode

description:
**Problem**
After the main decomposition, several loose ends remain:
1. New crates (\`beads-git\`, expanded \`beads-daemon\`, expanded \`beads-daemon-core\`) need AGENTS.md files describing their role and boundaries
2. \`beads_stateright_models/\` examples import \`beads_rs::daemon::*\` — needs updating to \`beads_daemon\` or \`beads_daemon_core\`
3. \`fuzz/fuzz_targets/\` import daemon internals (repl_message_decode.rs, wal_decode.rs) — needs updating
4. \`crates/beads-rs/src/model/\` has coupling to daemon types (see non-cli-daemon-coupling-inventory.md) — decide if model/ moves or stays
5. Remove compat shims from beads-rs once all consumers updated (\`pub use beads_git::*\` etc.)

**Design**
Fix each item. Most are import path changes. AGENTS.md files should follow the existing pattern (see \`crates/beads-core/src/AGENTS.md\` for example).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] AGENTS.md in \`crates/beads-git/\`, updated in \`crates/beads-daemon/\`, updated in \`crates/beads-daemon-core/\`
- [ ] Zero \`beads_rs::daemon::*\` imports in stateright models
- [ ] Zero \`beads_rs::daemon::*\` imports in fuzz targets
- [ ] \`model/\` module ownership decided and implemented
- [ ] All compat shims (\`pub use beads_git::*\` etc.) removed from beads-rs
- [ ] \`cargo check --workspace\` passes
- [ ] \`cargo test --workspace\` passes

**Files:** \`crates/beads-git/AGENTS.md\` (new), \`crates/beads-daemon/src/AGENTS.md\`, \`crates/beads-daemon-core/src/AGENTS.md\`, \`crates/beads_stateright_models/examples/*.rs\`, \`crates/fuzz/fuzz_targets/*.rs\`, \`crates/beads-rs/src/model/\`, \`crates/beads-rs/src/git/mod.rs\` (remove shim)
title: Update AGENTS.md files + fix stateright/fuzz/model daemon imports

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `MIGRATION.md` documents `skipped_no_remote` in terms of a missing remote push target, not only missing origin.
- [ ] The docs no longer imply the fleet execute loop can be reused unchanged for `--no-push` rehearsals.
- [ ] Rehearsal guidance matches actual `--no-push` semantics.
description:
**Problem**

the operator guide still drifts from actual behavior in two ways. It describes `skipped_no_remote` too narrowly as only the 'no origin' case, even though the implementation also returns it when origin exists but there is no remote `refs/heads/beads/store` to compare/push against. It also says operators can add `--no-push` to the fleet execute loop for a rehearsal, but that loop's own success gates and local-vs-remote equality checks are incompatible with a real local-only rewrite.

**Files**: MIGRATION.md
design:
**Design**
Tighten the operator guide so `skipped_no_remote` covers both missing-origin and missing-remote-store-ref cases, and split the rehearsal story from the pushed-cutover loop instead of implying the same verification gates apply to both. Keep the pushed-cutover loop strict; add a separate local-only rehearsal note or snippet that checks local strict-load success and origin non-mutation instead of local/remote equality.
title: Migration follow-up: fix remaining rollout docs for skipped_no_remote and no-push rehearsal loops

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `BeadPatch.status` is typed, not `Patch<String>`.
- [ ] Invalid status strings are rejected during IPC parsing with a clear validation error.
- [ ] Mutation engine no longer matches status strings.
description:
**Problem**
`src/daemon/ops.rs` defines `BeadPatch.status` as `Patch<String>`. The mutation engine parses strings into `WorkflowStatus` later, so invalid values flow deep into realtime planning and errors are ad-hoc. This keeps workflow invariants out of the type system and duplicates parsing logic.

**Files**
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs
design: Change `BeadPatch.status` to a typed patch (`Patch<WorkflowStatus>` or `Patch<Workflow>`). Provide serde mapping at the IPC boundary so JSON still uses `open|in_progress|closed`. Validate/parse in IPC and keep internal code fully typed. Update mutation engine and canonicalization to use the typed status directly; remove string matching.
title: BeadPatch.status should be typed (WorkflowStatus)

description:
**Problem**
Go beads prevents a specific anti-pattern: children explicitly depending on their parent via blocks/waits-for. This creates a deadlock because:
- Child can't start (parent is open)
- Parent can't close (children not done)

This is an LLM temporal reasoning trap. beads-rs needs this validation.

**How it works in Go beads**
- Detection in cmd/bd/dep.go lines 152-154 and 343-345
- Uses `isChildOf()` helper (lines 31-44)
- Checks hierarchical ID structure: "bd-abc.1" is child of "bd-abc"
- Blocks creation with clear error message
- Error: "Adding an explicit dependency would create a deadlock"
- Pure validation logic - no fix, just reject

**Detection algorithm**
```go
func isChildOf(childID, parentID string) bool {
    _, actualParentID, depth := types.ParseHierarchicalID(childID)
    if depth == 0 {
        return false // Not hierarchical
    }
    if actualParentID == parentID {
        return true // Immediate parent
    }
    return strings.HasPrefix(childID, parentID + ".")
}
```

**Design for Rust**
1. Add hierarchical ID parsing to BeadId in src/core/identity.rs
   - Parse "bd-abc.1.2" into (base, depth, parent)
   - Depth 0 = not hierarchical
2. Add is_child_of() method to BeadId
3. Add validation in dep add operation (daemon/ops/)
   - Check if from.is_child_of(to) for blocks/waits-for deps
   - Return clear error if detected
4. Add test cases for edge cases:
   - Immediate child: bd-abc.1 depends on bd-abc
   - Nested child: bd-abc.1.2 depends on bd-abc
   - Non-hierarchical: bd-abc depends on bd-xyz (ok)

**Design considerations**
- Which dep types need this check?
  * Blocks: yes (creates deadlock)
  * WaitsFor: yes (creates deadlock)
  * Parent: no (that's the hierarchy itself)
  * Others: no (informational)
- Should we check reverse (parent depending on child)? 
  * That's actually fine - parent blocks until child done
- Error message clarity for LLMs

**Design questions for human review**
- Should we detect transitive child relationships?
  * "bd-abc.1.2" is grandchild of "bd-abc"
  * Current Go code handles this via prefix check
- Should this be a warning or hard error?
  * Go: hard error (prevents creation)
  * Seems right - this is always wrong

**Files to study**
- tmp/beads/cmd/bd/dep.go - isChildOf helper, validation sites
- tmp/beads/internal/types/types.go - ParseHierarchicalID function
- src/core/identity.rs - BeadId structure

**Acceptance**
- [ ] BeadId supports hierarchical parsing
- [ ] is_child_of() method implemented and tested
- [ ] Dep add validates child-to-parent for blocks/waits-for
- [ ] Clear error message returned on violation
- [ ] Test coverage for immediate and nested children
- [ ] Cargo fmt, clippy, test pass
title: Add child-to-parent dependency deadlock detection
